name: Talysh
notes: Talysh (tolışi), a Northwestern Iranian language of the Caspian littoral in Azerbaijan and Iran. COMMITMENT: this map implements the AZERBAIJANI-BASED TALYSH LATIN ALPHABET (the 'Azerbaijan variant'), the script of the Talysh Wikipedia. Talysh is also written in Cyrillic and, in Iran, in a Perso-Arabic abjad; both are OUT OF SCOPE. A second, scholarly Latin romanisation (the háček variant: <c>=/t͡ʃ/, <j>=/j/, <g>=/ɡ/, <č ž š ǧ>) conflicts with this one on c/ç, j/y and q/g and is likewise not implemented. INPUT CONTRACT: lowercase Talysh text in the Azerbaijani-based Latin alphabet, diacritics intact (ç ə ğ ı ş ü) — note the Turkish-style dotted/dotless <i>/<ı> distinction is phonemic here. Azerbaijani letter values apply: <c> = /d͡ʒ/, <ç> = /t͡ʃ/, <j> = /ʒ/, <y> = /j/, <ş> = /ʃ/, <x> = /x/, <ğ> = /ɣ/, <ə> = the open vowel /a~æ/, <q> = /ɡ/. KNOWN GAP: Talysh HAS a phonemic uvular /q/, but the Azerbaijani-based alphabet provides no letter for it (<q> is /ɡ/, as in Azerbaijani), so /q/ is not recoverable from this orthography. [ʏ] (written <ü>) occurs in free variation with /u/, and /a/ is often palatalised to [æ]; both are handled in the allophone table.
